draft:
Dear Officer:

I am writing this letter in support of the EB-1 petition filed by Ms. Sofia Petrenko, an extraordinary leader in the consumer goods industry, particularly in digital marketing and business transformation.

I am Lisa Johnson, Vice Dean of Zenith Executive Education and the Ollie R. Thompson Associate Professor of Marketing at the Zenith School of Business. Over the past 25 years, I have served as a Professor of Marketing, conducting research and strategic projects in marketing across various industries, including consumer goods. I am currently the Vice Dean of Zenith Executive Education.

Prior to my current role, I served as the Vice Dean of the Zenith MBA Program for Executives from 2019 to 2022. This program is designed for mid-career professionals and executives who wish to earn an MBA while continuing to work full-time. My leadership in this program underscores my expertise in executive education and my ability to mentor and guide experienced professionals.

In addition to my administrative roles, I have been a faculty member at the Zenith School since 2000. I have taught courses in marketing strategy, consumer behavior, and brand management, among others. My research focuses on the role of emotions in consumer decision-making, and I have published numerous articles in leading academic journals on this topic.

My extensive academic and professional background has allowed me to work with many talented individuals, and Ms. Petrenko stands out as one of the brightest and most accomplished students I have had the privilege to teach.

I know Ms. Petrenko from my time teaching at Zenith, where she excelled academically and demonstrated exceptional intellectual curiosity. We collaborated on an independent study project, and our paths overlapped in several school-related events, including external speaker sessions and discussion panels. During these interactions, Ms. Petrenko's passion for marketing, her analytical thinking, and her ability to engage meaningfully with peers and experts alike consistently impressed me.

Ms. Petrenko's academic credentials are truly exemplary. Her degree in Economics from Hanover College and MBA from the Zenith School reflect her strong foundation in analytical rigor and strategic thinking. More importantly, her practical application of these skills in the real world has been extraordinary. Ms. Petrenko's business acumen, combined with her creativity and drive, is a direct reflection of the high standards associated with her educational background.

Ms. Petrenko has established a proven track record in consumer goods marketing, setting her apart as a leader in her field. From her internships at Dermatology Inc. and Molecule Venture Capital while at Zenith to her seven years at Unilever, Ms. Petrenko has consistently demonstrated her ability to transform marketing strategies and deliver measurable results. It came as no surprise to me—or others who know Ms. Petrenko—that she secured a Chief Marketing Officer role at just 30 years old shortly after graduating from Zenith.

At Unilever’s BabyCare Startup, Ms. Petrenko's leadership during a time of significant disruption from direct-to-consumer (DTC) brands was particularly noteworthy. She played a pivotal role in adapting BabyCare's digital marketing strategy, leveraging her understanding of consumer needs and industry trends. Her ability to lead through uncertainty and reposition BabyCare as a leader in the category reflects her exceptional talent and strategic mindset.

Ms. Petrenko is not only an expert in digital marketing but also a natural leader whose impact on the consumer goods industry is just beginning. Her hard work, natural talent, and unwavering passion for understanding and serving consumers set her apart from her peers. The sky is truly the limit for someone with Ms. Petrenko's unique combination of skills and dedication. I have no doubt that she will continue to make significant contributions to her industry for years to come.

In conclusion, I wholeheartedly recommend Ms. Sofia Petrenko as an extraordinary professional in her field. Her exceptional track record, academic excellence, and transformative leadership in the consumer goods industry make her a deserving candidate for this EB-1 petition.

If I can provide any further information, please contact me at any time.

Sincerely,

Lisa Johnson

Vice Dean, Zenith Executive Education

Ollie R. Thompson Associate Professor of Marketing, Zenith School of Business
final:
Dear USCIS Officer:

I am writing this letter to wholeheartedly recommend Ms. Sofia Petrenko as an individual of extraordinary ability in the field of consumer goods and digital strategy. I have known Ms. Petrenko since her time at the Zenith School of Business and have since followed her career with great interest, witnessing her remarkable achievements and contributions to the field.

For context, as the Vice Dean of Zenith Executive Education and the Ollie R. Thompson Associate Professor of Marketing at the Zenith School of the University of St. Petersburg, where I have served for over 24 years, I bring extensive expertise in marketing strategy, consumer behavior, and brand development. Throughout my career, I have specialized in researching how emotions influence consumer behavior and have shared this knowledge through teaching brand strategy, marketing strategy, and consumer insights. Prior to joining Zenith, I served as an Assistant Professor at Columbia University School of Business, and I recently completed a term as President of the Society for Consumer Psychology, further demonstrating my leadership in the field.

My extensive academic and professional background has allowed me to work with many talented individuals, and Ms. Petrenko stands out as one of the brightest and most accomplished students I have had the privilege of knowing.

Before joining the Zenith School of Business, Ms. Petrenko was pivotal at Unilever, where she spent seven years significantly impacting the company's digital marketing strategies. At Unilever’s BabyCare Startup, she led the adaptation to the rapidly evolving digital landscape, which was increasingly influenced by direct-to-consumer brands. Her leadership greatly contributed to repositioning BabyCare as a leader in its category, as she brought best-in-class digital marketing strategies from Silicon Valley and other startups to the large-scale operations of Unilever.

During her time with the BabyCare Startup team, Ms. Petrenko focused on several key aspects of digital marketing. She led initiatives such as consumer data acquisition, where innovative content marketing strategies were employed to gather email and personal data from consumers both in the United States and globally, achieving an impressive 60% success rate in data acquisition domestically. Additionally, she was instrumental in developing digital products, including the renowned BabyCare Club app-based loyalty program, which is well-regarded in the US and boasts millions of users. Her efforts in growth marketing and customer retention led to a doubling of online sales for BabyCare and related Unilever products. Her strategic insights and successes were so impactful that Unilever re-applied these digital marketing strategies to its other billion-dollar brands like RazorEdge and Flow.

Building on her impressive achievements at Unilever, Ms. Petrenko brought the same level of dedication and innovation to her studies at the Zenith School of Business. There, she excelled academically, displaying remarkable intellectual curiosity that set her apart. Collaborating closely with me on an independent study project, she demonstrated her ability to delve deeply into complex topics. Her active participation in various school-related events, including external speaker sessions and discussion panels, further showcased her passion for marketing and her analytical prowess. Through these interactions, she consistently exhibited her ability to engage meaningfully with peers and experts, as well as her eagerness to learn and contribute. Earning an MBA from Zenith, along with her degree in Economics from Hanover College, underscores her strong foundation in analytical rigor and strategic thinking. Her experiences at Zenith solidified her groundwork for future successes in the industry, highlighting her potential as a leader and innovator in marketing.

Following her academic achievements and hands-on experience at Zenith, Ms. Petrenko made an impressive transition into a high-level executive position as Chief Marketing Officer at ToyWorks, shortly after receiving her MBA. ToyWorks, a longstanding children's toy brand, generates over $10 million in sales across 35+ countries, with significant markets including early and special educational centers. Since assuming the CMO role in June 2022, Ms. Petrenko has also taken on the responsibilities of General Manager of Online Sales. Her strategic oversight significantly contributes to 80% of the company's revenue, with profits from over half of the business resting under her management. Her marketing acumen has led to ToyWorks' products being featured in prominent publications such as the Daily Journal, Trend, Home Style, and Entrepreneur.

In her capacity as CMO, Ms. Petrenko is responsible for crafting and executing marketing strategies across global markets, bolstering ToyWorks' presence in the competitive toy industry. Her rapid ascension to such a pivotal role highlights her remarkable leadership potential and expertise in the field. By January 2025, she was promoted to North America Sales Lead, extending her leadership to encompass all U.S. sales channels.

Beyond these achievements, Ms. Petrenko has initiated an incubation effort within ToyWorks to launch a new brand, FunBox. This endeavor highlights her entrepreneurial skills and strategic insight, as she works on securing $2 million in venture capital funding for FunBox. Once the investment is secured, Ms. Petrenko will take on the role of CEO and co-founder of FunBox. This step marks a significant milestone in her career, reflecting her ability to spearhead innovative initiatives and expand ToyWorks' portfolio in the competitive business environment.

Ms. Petrenko's impact on the field of marketing and consumer goods has been profound and transformative. Her ability to seamlessly integrate advanced digital strategies into established brands has set new standards and inspired similar initiatives across the industry. Through her upcoming venture with FunBox, Ms. Petrenko is poised to continue her trajectory of innovation and leadership, reinforcing her status as a dynamic force in shaping the future of consumer goods marketing. Her achievements serve as a benchmark for excellence, demonstrating the power of strategic vision combined with practical execution in driving industry advancements.

In summary, I unequivocally recommend Ms. Sofia Petrenko as an extraordinary professional in her field. Her remarkable track record, academic excellence, and transformative leadership in the consumer goods industry make her a deserving candidate for classification as an individual of extraordinary ability. If I can provide any further information, please contact me at any time.

Sincerely,



Lisa Johnson

Vice Dean, Zenith Executive Education
Ollie R. Thompson Associate Professor of Marketing, Zenith School of Business
[insert contact information]
